title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today's signal centers on moving safety checks closer to deployment: runtime compliance monitoring for agents, pre-release simulation of model behavior, and security controls for agentic systems. New evaluation work also targets higher-risk domains and misuse-relevant capabilities, while fresh research probes compute governance, privacy leakage, interpretability, and synthetic media detection.

items.title: Runtime Compliance Verification for AI Agents
items.why_it_matters: Shifts privacy and GDPR assurance from offline testing toward live enforcement of agent behavior.

items.title: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
items.why_it_matters: Adds a pre-deployment evaluation method using realistic interactions to surface safety failures earlier.

items.title: Securing the future of AI agents
items.why_it_matters: Shows how labs are adapting security controls and monitoring for increasingly autonomous agent systems.

items.title: Introducing LifeSciBench
items.why_it_matters: Provides expert-built evaluation for model performance on consequential life science research tasks.

items.title: TxBench-PP: Analyzing AI Agent Performance on Small-Molecule Preclinical Pharmacology
items.why_it_matters: Extends agent evaluation into drug-discovery decisions where errors or overclaiming can carry real-world risk.

items.title: A Multi-Domain Benchmark for Detecting AI-Generated Text-Rich Images from GPT-Image-2
items.why_it_matters: Targets provenance and fraud detection for synthetic documents containing sensitive or decision-relevant text.

items.title: Detecting Hidden ML Training With Zero-Overhead Telemetry
items.why_it_matters: Tests whether lightweight hardware telemetry could support enforceable AI compute monitoring and governance.

items.title: Reconstruction Limits for Repeated Differentially Private Aggregates
items.why_it_matters: Refines how practitioners assess privacy leakage from repeated differentially private query releases.

items.title: Confidence is Not Reliability: Rethinking MC Dropout in Brain Tumour Segmentation
items.why_it_matters: Highlights that uncertainty estimates can miss clinically dangerous failures in high-stakes AI deployment.

items.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
items.why_it_matters: Offers a new interpretability route by approximating transformer components with human-readable programs.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
